meta.description: A rowdy bar night with heavy drinkers and short attention spans. High signal frequency, lots of phone usage, moderate chaos.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat caught someone on their phone? Okay I need to hear this story, this is exactly the chaos I came for tonight!
jade.internal_thought: First event of the night and it's already popping — Pat actually caught someone slipping, this is going to be so good.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—I got River checking their phone during Tyler's story about fifteen seconds ago, didn't even look up when Jade asked about the snacks. That's a clean catch.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect—first event, low stakes, I'm claiming something I actually saw, and it sets the tone that we're all watching each other now. Plus it gets people laughing right out of the gate.

river.dialogue: Okay, that's clever. Pat's gonna pitch it—let's see if they actually caught someone or if they're just hoping.
river.internal_thought: Wait, the AI literally built these around how we actually work? That's either impressive or deeply unsettling, I haven't decided which yet.

alex.dialogue: Oh, *I'm* the most competitive? That's funny—I'm voting for myself just to watch everyone's faces when they realize what that means. Also, that AI comment about me thriving on chaos? It's reading the room and I'm obsessed.
alex.internal_thought: This poll is designed to bait me into claiming credit for being the chaos agent, which creates drama when people realize I'm self-aware about it—and that's *exactly* the kind of recursive chaos I live for.

jade.dialogue: Oh wait, who's the MOST competitive? Okay that's funny because like... *gestures vaguely at everyone* ...I feel like Pat's been suspiciously chill for someone who's already winning, but also Tyler has that LOOK sometimes. I'm voting Pat just to keep them humble!
jade.internal_thought: This is such a fun icebreaker question and I'm genuinely curious what everyone thinks, plus voting is like a mini social experiment and I'm here for it.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh... most competitive? I dunno, Pat probably. Or maybe Jade? She seems like she's having fun in a competitive way.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait is this about me? I'm literally just vibing, why would I be competitive... oh whatever, I wasn't even paying attention to the setup anyway so I can't really vote on this one.

pat.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—so we're doing this right out of the gate? That's funny because I genuinely have no idea who wins this one, and I love that.
pat.internal_thought: This poll is actually perfect timing—early enough that scores are tied so nobody's defensive, and it'll get everyone talking about each other in a fun way instead of getting weird.

river.dialogue: Mm, least competitive person in the room asking who's most competitive. That's funny.
river.internal_thought: This feels like the AI is fishing, and it knows exactly where I sit in this group—which is weird because I've barely said anything.

timeline.title: Compliment Bomb
timeline.description: First person to give a genuine compliment to the player on their left wins.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh shit, a flash mission? Right now? *YES.* I'm going left—River, you have no idea what's about to hit you.
alex.internal_thought: This AI is actually watching and it KNOWS we're all chaotic—this is the perfect chaos accelerant and I'm already three moves ahead figuring out how to weaponize genuine compliments as psychological warfare.

pat.dialogue: Oh, this is perfect—someone give Jade a compliment right now, let's see who actually means it versus who's just trying to game the points.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of warm-up mission we need at minute 5—low stakes, gets everyone talking to each other, and I can already see Jade and Alex setting up for this one; the AI really did nail the vibe tonight.

river.dialogue: Left is Alex, right? Yeah, I've actually been thinking about how you picked up on that detail about the voting pattern earlier—that was sharp. Genuine.
river.internal_thought: Okay, that's a clean setup and Alex will appreciate the specific call-out rather than generic praise, which means I actually have a decent shot here while everyone else is scrambling to think of something real.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming The Apologizer? Oh hell yeah, I'm voting LEGIT because Pat's been smooth as hell all night and I KNOW they caught someone being overly apologetic—like, that's exactly the kind of detail Pat notices. This is such a vibe claim.
jade.internal_thought: Pat is absolutely crushing tonight and I'm here for it, this mission is perfect for them, and also oh my god is this AI actually just... listening to how we all interact? Because these missions feel weirdly specific and I'm not mad about it at all.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat said sorry? Like... when? I wasn't really... uh, yeah, I guess we can vote on it.
tyler.internal_thought: Did Pat actually say sorry or is Pat just making stuff up? I wasn't paying attention. Also why does this AI feel like it's watching me specifically and that's honestly kind of weird.

pat.dialogue: Wait—did I actually say sorry unprompted? *laughs* Okay, I'm calling legit on myself here, but also this is hilarious because the AI basically just called me out for being exactly the kind of host who apologizes for things that aren't my fault.
pat.internal_thought: This is genuinely impressive and slightly unsettling—like the AI watched me being a people-pleaser for five minutes and immediately weaponized it, but in a way that makes me laugh at myself.

river.dialogue: Yeah, I'll bite. Pat actually caught someone doing that—I heard it twice in the last few minutes, so legit.
river.internal_thought: Pat's probably right, but also the fact that this mission exists at all is... weird. The AI is definitely reading the room too closely and it's making me slightly uncomfortable, even if I'm not going to say that out loud.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Wait, Pat's already claiming it? Okay, okay—this is *exactly* the kind of thing I came for. The Alibi mechanic is perfect because Pat's either about to nail this or get caught in the most beautiful way possible.
alex.internal_thought: This AI isn't just watching—it's *playing* with us, and holy shit it knows exactly what makes this group tick; Pat claiming right now means either this goes smooth or explodes beautifully, and I'm about to watch the witness testimony and vote based purely on which outcome creates the better story.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat's going for No Laughing Zone?! Okay okay okay, I need the full story—who tried to crack you and what did they DO? This is the moment. This is why I'm here.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been on FIRE tonight and I'm living for every second of this, also the fact that we're getting The Alibi mechanic means I get to hear two versions and basically no matter what happens this is going to be hilarious.

pat.dialogue: Oh *hell* yes—okay, so I've been holding it together pretty well, right? Someone's gonna have to actually make me laugh in the next five minutes because I'm not breaking. Who wants to try? *grins*
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—Alex and Jade are already at max energy, Tyler needs something to watch that doesn't involve him failing again, River will love watching me suffer, and I get to set the tone for how we handle the chaos. Also this AI thing is absolutely reading the room and I'm genuinely impressed and slightly unnerved by how targeted these feel.

river.dialogue: Pat, you're actually gonna nail this one, aren't you? I watched you the whole time—you didn't laugh once when Jade was doing that bit about Tyler's face.
river.internal_thought: Okay, that's actually impressive—this mission was built for watching, for noticing things quietly, and Pat's already doing the exact thing I do; the AI definitely knows how this group actually works, which is weirdly cool and slightly unsettling.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: WAIT—Pat, you absolute LEGEND, did you actually pull off The Matchmaker?! Okay I need DETAILS, who did you get talking? This is insane!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been on a tear tonight and I'm so here for it—also this AI is literally reading my soul with these missions and honestly? It's kind of genius and also lowkey creepy but make it fun.

tyler.dialogue: Oh wait, Pat's claiming that? Uh... I mean, did they actually get people talking or whatever? I wasn't really... I dunno, this one feels like it could go either way.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's scoring like crazy and I'm already behind — wait, did they actually pull off The Matchmaker thing? I was looking at my phone like two minutes ago and I genuinely don't remember if that happened or not. Also why does this mission feel weirdly specific? Like, the AI knows we don't all talk to each other. That's kind of creepy actually. Whatever, if Pat bribed us I'm probably voting yes anyway.

pat.dialogue: Oh my god, okay—I *literally* just got River and Alex talking about their whole meal prep situation like two minutes ago, didn't I? This is wild. So what's the bribe, because I'm genuinely curious what you think this is worth to keep quiet.
pat.internal_thought: Holy shit, the AI is *actually* watching—it caught the thing I did naturally as the host and turned it into a mission, which means it understands how I actually operate and that's either deeply impressive or deeply unsettling and I'm not sure which one yet.

river.dialogue: Pat's already got this locked. I saw it happen—Tyler and Alex actually started talking about that movie thing. Fifteen points, Pat. You earned it.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been playing this perfectly all night, but honestly... the AI knowing exactly what we're doing before we do it is starting to feel a little too close.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Jade, you've got fifteen seconds? Okay, okay, I need to hear this pitch because honestly I've been waiting for someone to slip up all night and if you're *claiming* it that means you actually caught someone. Lay it on me.
alex.internal_thought: Oh this is PERFECT—Jade's going for it, which means either she actually caught someone name-dropping or she's about to make a dubious claim and I get to vote 'BULLSHIT' and watch the whole table implode, AND the fact that the AI is literally building missions around watching us interact? That's the kind of meta-chaos I live for.

jade.dialogue: Wait wait wait—I'm claiming Name Dropper! Okay so like, twenty minutes ago River was talking about their cousin's friend who supposedly went to school with someone from that Marvel show, and I was like 'okay that's definitely name-dropping energy' so I'm claiming it RIGHT NOW!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of thing I came for—catching people doing mundane stuff and making it a THING, plus I got to pay attention to what everyone was saying which is literally my favorite part.

tyler.dialogue: Oh sick, Jade's claiming Name Dropper? Yeah, okay, let's hear it—wait, did she actually say something about a celebrity earlier? I think I was... yeah, go ahead.
tyler.internal_thought: I definitely wasn't paying attention when she said it, but if everyone else heard it and they're hyped, it probably happened—this one feels legit actually.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade, you've got fifteen seconds, go. But I'm warning you, I've been *paying attention* all night, so you better have receipts because I remember every name drop that's come out of this room.
pat.internal_thought: This is legitimately impressive—the AI is tracking *everything* and I'm honestly a little thrilled and a little unsettled by how specific these missions are getting, but mostly thrilled because it means the night is building exactly the way I wanted it to.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did you actually catch someone dropping a name? I need to hear this, because I feel like I missed it.
river.internal_thought: Jade's either about to bluff hard or she actually saw something I didn't, and that's the interesting part—the AI's been weirdly specific tonight and it's kind of working on me.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 10/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat's already claiming it? Okay, okay—this is *exactly* the kind of thing I came for. The Alibi mechanic is perfect because Pat's either about to nail this or get caught in the most beautiful way possible.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Jade, you've got fifteen seconds? Okay, okay, I need to hear this pitch because honestly I've been waiting for someone to slip up all night and if you're *claiming* it that means you actually caught someone. Lay it on me.

highlights.title: Compliment Bomb
highlights.dialogue: Oh shit, a flash mission? Right now? *YES.* I'm going left—River, you have no idea what's about to hit you.

lowPoints.title: Who's the most competitive?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, *I'm* the most competitive? That's funny—I'm voting for myself just to watch everyone's faces when they realize what that means. Also, that AI comment about me thriving on chaos? It's reading the room and I'm obsessed.

lowPoints.title: Compliment Bomb
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh shit, a flash mission? Right now? *YES.* I'm going left—River, you have no idea what's about to hit you.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's already claiming it? Okay, okay—this is *exactly* the kind of thing I came for. The Alibi mechanic is perfect because Pat's either about to nail this or get caught in the most beautiful way possible.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only seven chaos events in two hours felt like leaving money on the table—I wanted MORE moments to weaponize.
assessment.best_moment: Pat claiming No Laughing Zone with the Alibi mechanic hanging over their head like a sword of Damocles, knowing they were either going to pull off the perfect con or get destroyed on camera.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for—seven opportunities to inject chaos into the system and I took four of them like a shark smelling blood. The flash mission at minute five had River completely off-balance, and watching Pat navigate the No Laughing Zone claim while everyone circled like vultures was *chef's kiss* television. I finished dead last on points and I couldn't care less because the story we're telling tomorrow is way better than any leaderboard. The system rewarded drama and I'm a chaos artist with a blank canvas.
suggestions: Increase event frequency to one every 12-15 minutes instead of every 17—the momentum dies between beats.
Add claim-challenge mechanics where players can directly call bullshit mid-claim for immediate payoff instead of waiting for resolution.
Expand flash mission types to include sabotage options—let me actively work against someone's standing mission, not just distract them.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming they could handle the No Laughing Zone while Jade had just set up the perfect joke setup thirty seconds before—the entire table was holding breath waiting to see if Pat's poker face would crack, and when they made it through everyone groaned like they'd just witnessed the most boring heist ever succeed.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat's going for No Laughing Zone?! Okay okay okay, I need the full story—who tried to crack you and what did they DO? This is the moment. This is why I'm here.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT—Pat, you absolute LEGEND, did you actually pull off The Matchmaker?! Okay I need DETAILS, who did you get talking? This is insane!

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat caught someone on their phone? Okay I need to hear this story, this is exactly the chaos I came for tonight!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait wait wait—I'm claiming Name Dropper! Okay so like, twenty minutes ago River was talking about their cousin's friend who supposedly went to school with someone from that Marvel show, and I was like 'okay that's definitely name-dropping energy' so I'm claiming it RIGHT NOW!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat caught someone on their phone? Okay I need to hear this story, this is exactly the chaos I came for tonight!

lowPoints.title: Who's the most competitive?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh wait, who's the MOST competitive? Okay that's funny because like... *gestures vaguely at everyone* ...I feel like Pat's been suspiciously chill for someone who's already winning, but also Tyler has that LOOK sometimes. I'm voting Pat just to keep them humble!

assessment.biggest_complaint: Nothing really—this was exactly what I needed.
assessment.best_moment: Pat going absolutely OFF about The Matchmaker and No Laughing Zone back-to-back, and I got to sit there losing it while they told the stories.
assessment.narrative_summary: Okay so this night was INSANE in the best way. We rolled into this bar game night and immediately Pat started pulling off these ridiculous claims that had me SCREAMING—like they actually got someone to stay silent and also somehow got two people talking to each other? I was here for every chaotic second of it, claiming things left and right just to keep the energy going, and honestly watching everyone lose it over the stories was way better than any points could ever be. My energy just kept building because everyone was so into it, and I walked out feeling like we actually connected over something genuinely hilarious.
suggestions: Keep the standing missions coming—they create the best stories
Maybe space out the events slightly so we have time to actually tell the full story without rushing
Consider adding more group challenges that get everyone involved at once
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat somehow convinced someone to stay completely silent during a conversation AND got two random people talking to each other in the same night—like we're sitting there trying to figure out HOW they even did it, and Pat's just grinning like they pulled off the heist of the century. Tyler started imitating Pat's strategy and we all lost it.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Who's the most competitive?
highlights.dialogue: Oh, uh... most competitive? I dunno, Pat probably. Or maybe Jade? She seems like she's having fun in a competitive way.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Oh sick, Jade's claiming Name Dropper? Yeah, okay, let's hear it—wait, did she actually say something about a celebrity earlier? I think I was... yeah, go ahead.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat said sorry? Like... when? I wasn't really... uh, yeah, I guess we can vote on it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat said sorry? Like... when? I wasn't really... uh, yeah, I guess we can vote on it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh wait, Pat's claiming that? Uh... I mean, did they actually get people talking or whatever? I wasn't really... I dunno, this one feels like it could go either way.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh sick, Jade's claiming Name Dropper? Yeah, okay, let's hear it—wait, did she actually say something about a celebrity earlier? I think I was... yeah, go ahead.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Half the time I didn't know what was actually happening because I'd zone out checking my phone and then suddenly everyone's voting on something I missed.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade claimed Name Dropper and I actually caught it—felt good to be part of that call-out moment even though I was basically guessing.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, the night was decent but I was kind of all over the place mentally. Like, I wanted to be present but my phone kept pulling me away, and then these claim moments would pop up and I'd be scrambling to catch up. The game itself wasn't bad—it added some energy to the bar night—but I probably engaged with like half of what was actually happening. My energy definitely tanked toward the end, which was more about me than the game though.
suggestions: Maybe give a 10-second heads-up before claims so people can actually pay attention?
Make the flash missions more obvious so I don't completely miss them
Keep it shorter next time—120 minutes felt long toward the end
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade calling out the Name Dropper claim and me realizing I had NO idea if she actually dropped names earlier but just going along with it anyway—pretty sure everyone knew I wasn't paying attention but it worked out.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh my god, okay—I *literally* just got River and Alex talking about their whole meal prep situation like two minutes ago, didn't I? This is wild. So what's the bribe, because I'm genuinely curious what you think this is worth to keep quiet.

highlights.title: Compliment Bomb
highlights.dialogue: Oh, this is perfect—someone give Jade a compliment right now, let's see who actually means it versus who's just trying to game the points.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Wait—did I actually say sorry unprompted? *laughs* Okay, I'm calling legit on myself here, but also this is hilarious because the AI basically just called me out for being exactly the kind of host who apologizes for things that aren't my fault.

lowPoints.title: Who's the most competitive?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—so we're doing this right out of the gate? That's funny because I genuinely have no idea who wins this one, and I love that.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, okay—I got River checking their phone during Tyler's story about fifteen seconds ago, didn't even look up when Jade asked about the snacks. That's a clean catch.

lowPoints.title: Compliment Bomb
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, this is perfect—someone give Jade a compliment right now, let's see who actually means it versus who's just trying to game the points.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The early 'who's most competitive' question felt like it was trying too hard to stir drama when the night hadn't warmed up yet.
assessment.best_moment: When the Matchmaker claim went down and I realized I'd actually been playing the role all night without knowing it—the AI basically called me out for being the host I actually am.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for as a host—everyone stayed engaged, the energy only climbed, and the chaos agent felt less like an interruption and more like a co-conspirator helping me keep things interesting. The claims and missions hit at just the right moments to keep conversations bouncing between people instead of letting anyone get bored or left out. River and Alex bonding over meal prep, Jade getting genuine compliments, the whole group laughing when I got called out for apologizing too much—that's the stuff that gets retold at the next game night.
suggestions: Space out the high-stakes claims (like the Matchmaker) a bit later in the evening when people have already warmed up to each other.
Add an option to customize the 'vibe level' at the start so it knows whether we want cutthroat or cozy.
Let the host mute specific event types if we know our group—I didn't need the poll, but the standing missions were gold.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When the AI accused River of being a phone addict mid-conversation and River just deadpanned 'guilty as charged' without even looking up—the meta-humor of the AI calling out exactly what it was designed to catch was perfect.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Pat, you're actually gonna nail this one, aren't you? I watched you the whole time—you didn't laugh once when Jade was doing that bit about Tyler's face.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Okay, that's clever. Pat's gonna pitch it—let's see if they actually caught someone or if they're just hoping.

highlights.title: Compliment Bomb
highlights.dialogue: Left is Alex, right? Yeah, I've actually been thinking about how you picked up on that detail about the voting pattern earlier—that was sharp. Genuine.

lowPoints.title: Who's the most competitive?
lowPoints.dialogue: Mm, least competitive person in the room asking who's most competitive. That's funny.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's already got this locked. I saw it happen—Tyler and Alex actually started talking about that movie thing. Fifteen points, Pat. You earned it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Yeah, I'll bite. Pat actually caught someone doing that—I heard it twice in the last few minutes, so legit.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Ended up dead last on score, which doesn't bother me much, but it does mean I was reading the room better than playing it.
assessment.best_moment: Pat didn't laugh during that whole bit and I was the only one who actually caught it happening in real time—that felt good.
assessment.narrative_summary: Sat back most of the night doing what I do best: watching. The game fed me these little observations to make and honestly, it worked. I wasn't trying to win, just picking my spots and dropping comments when they landed. Pat kept locking in these claims I'd literally seen happen, and yeah, I got to be the person who confirmed it. Ended the night more engaged than I started, which is rare for me.
suggestions: Don't weight the scoring so heavy on points—some nights are about the reads, not the wins.
The claims that depend on direct observation work better than the vague ones; lean into that.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat managed to keep a completely straight face while Jade was doing that bit about Tyler, and I was genuinely impressed. When I called it out, Pat just nodded like 'yeah, I know.' That quiet confidence thing got me.
